calendarNotes:
Palm Beach County School District begins the 2025-2026 school year on August 11, 2025. Spring Break runs March 16–20, 2025, and the school year ends May 29, 2025. In 2025-2026, Palm Beach County shares the same Spring Break window as both Miami-Dade and Broward County, making the entire South Florida tri-county area on break simultaneously. This alignment doesn't happen every year — in some years Palm Beach breaks a few days earlier or later than its southern neighbors. The week of March 16–20 is peak season along the Palm Beach coastline, and hotels from Boca Raton to Jupiter fill quickly once all three counties announce the same break date.

Palm Beach County is geographically diverse in ways that affect school planning. The county stretches from affluent coastal communities like Boca Raton and Palm Beach to rural agricultural towns like Belle Glade and Pahokee near Lake Okeechobee. Schools in the western, inland areas of the county serve a large migrant and agricultural workforce, which can affect attendance patterns during harvest season — typically October through December. Families relocating from other states to either the coastal or inland portions of the county will find substantially different school environments and demographic contexts, often within the same district.

Florida's hurricane emergency closure rules apply to Palm Beach County as they do to all Florida districts. Palm Beach's location at the upper end of the South Florida coastline makes it slightly less exposed to the most intense hurricane activity than Miami-Dade, but direct hits are not uncommon. The district banks makeup days into its calendar structure. Families planning late-May travel should consider whether any storm closures earlier in the year might require extending the school year past the May 27 planned last day.

Palm Beach County's school choice and magnet program system is less expansive than Miami-Dade's or Broward's, but the district operates a number of specialized STEM, arts, and language programs at select campuses. Families interested in program-specific enrollment should contact the district's school choice office in the fall — applications for the most popular programs typically close by January for the following school year.
